note: Hand-curated kid-friendly food + activity spots in Bay Area cities where OpenStreetMap coverage is thin. Names and locations are approximate to real downtown blocks; nothing is verified. Treat this as a STARTER set — open each entry's url, confirm hours/menu, then flip verified:true. Goal is to give every city a reasonable seed list rather than have plans repeat the same handful of spots.

spots.name: Dish Dash
spots.note: Mediterranean small plates and grills on Murphy Ave. Reliable family weekend lunch.

spots.name: Hobee's Restaurant
spots.note: South Bay breakfast institution — blueberry coffee cake, kid menus, big tables.

spots.name: Eureka!
spots.note: Casual American burgers + fries on Murphy Ave. Patio when weather is good.

spots.name: Falafel Stop
spots.note: Cheap, fast falafel and shawarma. Small but reliable.

spots.name: Cocola Bakery
spots.note: European-style bakery; good post-park stop with toddlers.

spots.name: Alexander's Steakhouse
spots.note: High-end steakhouse — kid menu available; better for older kids.

spots.name: The Counter
spots.note: Build-your-own burger chain. Easy with picky kids.

spots.name: Hot Pot City
spots.note: All-you-can-eat hot pot. Kids enjoy cooking their own.

spots.name: Crepevine
spots.note: Sweet and savory crepes; reliable family brunch.

spots.name: Hobee's Original
spots.note: The original Hobee's — family weekend brunch institution.

spots.name: Le Boulanger
spots.note: Local bakery / cafe chain. Good post-park lunch.

spots.name: Sushi Tomi
spots.note: Long-running Castro Street sushi spot.

spots.name: The Fish Market
spots.note: Casual seafood — kids menu, reliable for groups.

spots.name: Donut Wheel
spots.note: Classic donut shop. Quick weekend morning stop with kids.

spots.name: Tin Pot Creamery
spots.note: Local small-batch ice cream. Classic post-museum / post-park stop.

spots.name: Coupa Café
spots.note: Venezuelan-leaning cafe with arepas and good coffee. Kid-friendly.

spots.name: Tacolicious
spots.note: Casual tacos. Easy with kids; chips and rice fix most picky-eater issues.

spots.name: Calafia Cafe
spots.note: Town & Country location. Healthy seasonal menu, family-friendly.

spots.name: Pizzeria Delfina
spots.note: Margherita and seasonal Neapolitan pizzas. Reliable kid order.

spots.name: The Curious Café
spots.note: Downtown brunch + lunch. Small, lines on weekends.

spots.name: Ramen Iroha
spots.note: Reliable ramen in downtown San Mateo. Kids OK with udon if not into ramen.

spots.name: The Fish Market — San Mateo
spots.note: Long-running family seafood. Big tables, kids menu.

spots.name: Stella Alpina Osteria
spots.note: Long-running Burlingame Italian. Family-friendly, fresh pasta.

spots.name: The Cheesecake Factory — Burlingame
spots.note: Big menu, no surprises. Easy default with picky kids.

spots.name: Donato Enoteca
spots.note: Pasta + Italian wines. Good with older kids.

spots.name: Vesta
spots.note: Wood-fired pizzas in downtown Redwood City. Family-friendly patio.

spots.name: Original Joe's
spots.note: Downtown San Jose institution since 1956. Big portions, family-friendly.

spots.name: Falafel Drive-In
spots.note: Long-running drive-in style falafel shop. Cheap and quick.

spots.name: The Old Spaghetti Factory — San Jose
spots.note: Big tables, kid menu, easy default for families.

spots.name: Bill's Cafe
spots.note: Long-running San Jose breakfast diner. Kid-friendly.

spots.name: Habana Cuba
spots.note: Long-running Cuban restaurant near SJSU.

spots.name: Bombay Garden
spots.note: South Asian buffet. Easy with mixed-age kids.

spots.name: Mio Vicino
spots.note: Casual Italian — pasta, pizza, kid menus.

spots.name: Buon Appetito
spots.note: Long-running downtown Hayward Italian.

spots.name: Sol Food
spots.note: Bright, lively Puerto Rican spot. Kid-friendly tostones and rice.

spots.name: Marin Joe's
spots.note: Marin institution. Big menu, family-friendly, classic vibes.

spots.name: Joe's Taco Lounge
spots.note: Quirky neighborhood taco lounge — kids enjoy the lucha libre vibe.

spots.name: Avatar's Punjabi Burritos
spots.note: Curry-stuffed burritos. Quick, popular, kids like the simple options.

spots.name: Eureka! — Walnut Creek
spots.note: Family-friendly burgers + fries.

spots.name: Pizza Antica
spots.note: Reliable Cal-Italian. Kids menu, patio at Broadway Plaza.

spots.name: Bakesale Betty
spots.note: Telegraph Ave fried chicken sandwich. Take-out, eat at the painted ironing-board tables.

spots.name: Fenton's Creamery
spots.note: Piedmont Ave institution. Massive sundaes, vintage feel — top-tier kid stop.

spots.name: Homeroom
spots.note: All mac and cheese, all the time. Kid-favorite.

spots.name: Cheese Board Pizza
spots.note: One pizza of the day, vegetarian, sold by the slice. Kids love the big tables and live music.

spots.name: Saul's Restaurant & Delicatessen
spots.note: Classic Berkeley deli. Latkes, matzoh ball soup, big tables.

spots.name: Top Dog
spots.note: Cheap hot dogs near campus. Kid-favorite quick stop.

spots.name: Burma Superstar
spots.note: Inner Richmond institution. Tea leaf salad, samusa soup. Family-friendly.

spots.name: Mama's on Washington Square
spots.note: Iconic SF brunch. Long line on weekends.

spots.name: Tartine Bakery
spots.note: Iconic Mission bakery. Lines move fast. Kid-friendly pastries.

spots.name: Bi-Rite Creamery
spots.note: Salted caramel + Sam's Sundae. Lines all summer; pair with Dolores Park.

spots.name: Palo Alto Junior Museum & Zoo
spots.note: Indoor science museum + small outdoor zoo. Great for ages 0-8.

spots.name: Shoreline Park
spots.note: Lake, paddleboats, sailing center, golf course, and a long, stroller-friendly path.

spots.name: Stevens Creek Trail (Cupertino)
spots.note: Long paved creekside trail. Stroller and bike friendly, lots of access points.

spots.name: Sunnyvale Baylands Park
spots.note: Big open lawns, two playgrounds, picnic areas. Bay views.

spots.name: Emma Prusch Farm Park
spots.note: City farm with chickens, peacocks, big lawns. Free, kid-favorite.

spots.name: McInnis Park
spots.note: Mini-golf, batting cages, big playground, picnic.

spots.name: Civic Park
spots.note: Big playground, creek-side path, picnic area near the library.

spots.name: Berkeley Ironworks
spots.note: Touchstone gym with kids climbing area, day pass, gear rental.

spots.name: Mission Cliffs
spots.note: Touchstone gym in the Mission. Big top-rope and bouldering walls; kid passes.

spots.name: Great Western Power Co.
spots.note: Massive Touchstone gym in a converted power station. Kid-friendly with a day pass.

spots.name: Sky High Sports — San Jose
spots.note: Big trampoline park. Reserve a slot, wear grip socks. Energy burner for school-age + tween.

spots.name: Rockin' Jump — Concord
spots.note: Trampoline + dodgeball + foam pit. Reserve ahead.

spots.name: Get Air — Belmont
spots.note: Trampolines, ninja warrior course, foam pit. Toddler-only sessions during the week.

spots.name: Bowlero Cupertino
spots.note: Family bowling with bumpers, food at the lane, arcade.

spots.name: Diablo Lanes
spots.note: Reliable family bowling alley. Bumpers for younger kids.

spots.name: Color Me Mine — Palo Alto
spots.note: Pick a piece, paint it, pick up later. Good rainy-day activity.

spots.name: Color Me Mine — Burlingame
spots.note: Burlingame Avenue paint-your-own-pottery shop.

spots.name: Iza Ramen
spots.note: Ramen + small plates. Kid-friendly udon options.

spots.name: Pasta Pomodoro — Foster City
spots.note: Bay Area Italian chain. Kid menu, easy weeknight option.

spots.name: Buca di Beppo — San Bruno
spots.note: Big shared platters. Designed for family group dinners.

spots.name: Taco Bell Cantina — Pacifica
spots.note: Iconic beachfront Taco Bell. Watch surfers, eat on the patio. Pair with Pacifica Pier walk.

spots.name: Pacifica Pier
spots.note: Long fishing pier and an easy beach walk. Stroller-friendly path along the seawall.

spots.name: Skate One — Westlake
spots.note: Indoor roller skating. Family sessions on weekend afternoons.

spots.name: Stoneridge Shopping Center
spots.note: Big mall with kid-friendly food court and indoor space — fall-back rainy-day plan.

spots.name: Shadow Cliffs Regional Park
spots.note: East Bay Regional Park with a swimming lake, beach, and easy trails. Lifeguarded in summer.

spots.name: Emerald Glen Park
spots.note: Multiple play structures + summer water spray. Free.

spots.name: Todos Santos Plaza
spots.note: Downtown Concord plaza. Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday farmers market with live music in summer.

spots.name: Helen Putnam Regional Park
spots.note: Easy 2-mile loop with kid-friendly elevation, big oaks, picnic.

spots.name: Stafford Lake Bike Park
spots.note: Pump tracks and skill loops for kids on bikes. Lake nearby.

spots.name: Bar Bocce
spots.note: Wood-fired pizza on the bay with a bocce court. Kid-friendly outdoors.

spots.name: Fish.
spots.note: Cash-only seafood at the docks. Kids OK at the picnic tables.

spots.name: Presidio Tunnel Tops
spots.note: Outpost play area with rope climbs, slides, and sand pit. Bay views, very stroller-friendly.

spots.name: Koret Children's Quarter Playground
spots.note: Iconic SF kid park: rope tower, big slide, vintage carousel ($) next door.

spots.name: Yerba Buena Children's Garden
spots.note: Free outdoor area at Yerba Buena Gardens; carousel, ice rink in winter, bowling at Yerba Buena Center for the Arts complex.

spots.name: Cake Mix Studio
spots.note: Decorate a pre-baked cake or cupcake with all the toppings. Parties on weekends.

spots.name: Cantor Arts Center
spots.note: Free Stanford art museum + the Rodin sculpture garden outside. Strollers welcome.
